# Source basis for this addendum

Last refreshed: 2026-04-20.

Use these as source anchors, not as universal laws. The hypotheses still need primary validation against the target account, niche, and audience.

## Official platform / product sources

- TikTok Help: How TikTok recommends content — user interactions such as likes, shares, comments, full watches and skips; content information such as sounds, hashtags and views; TikTok Search weighs how well content matches the search inquiry.  
  https://support.tiktok.com/en/using-tiktok/exploring-videos/how-tiktok-recommends-content
- YouTube Creators: creators should accurately present videos with titles/descriptions/thumbnails, and Shorts creators should think carefully about the first 1–2 seconds.  
  https://www.youtube.com/creators/
- YouTube Help: Shorts views changed from 2025-03-31 to count play/replay starts; Engaged views remain the metric for viewers who chose to continue watching.  
  https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/10059070
- Instagram Blog: Ranking explained — Reels ranking uses activity such as liked, saved, reshared, commented-on and recently engaged-with Reels.  
  https://about.instagram.com/blog/announcements/instagram-ranking-explained
- Meta Newsroom: Trial Reels let creators test Reels with non-followers first before sharing with followers.  
  https://about.fb.com/news/2024/12/trial-reels-try-content-non-followers-first-see-what-perfoms-best/
- TikTok Creative Center / Creative Insights: ad-context creative patterns and trend resources. Useful as inspiration, not organic proof.  
  https://ads.tiktok.com/business/creativecenter/pc/en

## Academic / research sources

- Gernsbacher, M. A. (2015), "Video Captions Benefit Everyone" — review noting 100+ empirical studies that captioning improves comprehension, attention and memory.  
  https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5214590/
- Farace et al. (2025/2026), "Standing Out While Fitting In: Visual Design of Text Overlays in Social Media Communication" — text overlay engagement depends on image dynamism, text size and centrality; large central overlays over dynamic images can hurt engagement.  
  https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/00222429251322773
- Wang, Ding & Hu (2025), "The impact of face presence in user-generated videos on consumer engagement" — moderate face presence around 30–40% of frames and early face presence can be effective, especially for smaller creators.  
  https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0167811625000096
- Berger & Milkman (2012), "What Makes Online Content Viral?" — high-arousal emotions such as awe, anger and anxiety are more viral than low-arousal emotions, controlling for other factors.  
  https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1509/jmr.10.0353

## Interpretation principle

The sources support the direction of the research model, but they do not prove any single tactic will work for a specific creator. Treat each item below as a hypothesis to validate.
